page.title: CSRF Protection
page.lead: CSRF (Cross-Site Request Forgery) is an attack where a malicious site tricks an authenticated user's browser into executing unwanted actions in your application.

importance.title: Why is mitigation important?
importance.description: If you do not validate CSRF tokens, an attacker can force operations like transfers, password changes, or profile updates using the victim's active session.
importance.warn: CSRF is especially critical when using cookie-based authentication because the browser sends cookies automatically.

middleware.title: Basic csrf() middleware
middleware.description: Use the built-in <mark class="docs-highlight">csrf()</mark> middleware to protect endpoints against CSRF attacks.

howItWorks.title: How it works internally
items: Issues a CSRF cookie when missing (including first GET/HEAD/OPTIONS requests and failed protected requests).
For state-changing methods (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE), it validates the token received in header/body against the cookie value.
Validates <mark class="docs-highlight">Origin</mark>/<mark class="docs-highlight">Referer</mark> in protected requests, and requires Referer in HTTPS when Origin is missing.
Rejects duplicated CSRF values in headers to avoid token parsing ambiguity.

templates.title: Template example (Express Handlebars)
templates.description: In server-side rendering, pass the CSRF token to the view and include it as a hidden field in forms.

ajax.title: CSRF in fetch/AJAX
ajax.description: For AJAX requests, send the same token in headers using the name defined in <mark class="docs-highlight">headerNames</mark>.
ajax.tip: Keep the CSRF token aligned across cookie, hidden field, and header to avoid false validation positives.
